--- Commit Message ---
LU-13417 mdd: set default LMV on ROOT

To balance MDT usage, set default LMV on ROOT if it's not set. The
default stripe offset is "-1", and default stripe count is "1". Then
directory created by "mkdir" under ROOT will be scattered on all MDTs
by usage.

Add sanity 0e.

Signed-off-by: Lai Siyao <lai.siyao@whamcloud.com>
Signed-off-by: Andreas Dilger <adilger@whamcloud.com>
Signed-off-by: Hongchao Zhang <hongchao@whamcloud.com>
Change-Id: I7a6c752256225b8d065b2c304c4725268df28045
Reviewed-on: https://review.whamcloud.com/38553
Tested-by: jenkins <devops@whamcloud.com>
Tested-by: Maloo <maloo@whamcloud.com>

--- Message (Andreas Dilger, 2020-06-19 18:26:54.000000000) ---
Patch Set 7:

HongChao, when you get a chance, can you please look through the test failures. I expect many of them are due to creating the top-level $DIR/$tdir somewhere other than MDT0000.

Those tests will need an explicit "lfs mkdir -i0 $DIR/$tdir" in order to pass. Please include a one-line comment with such cases to make it clear this is only for LU-13417 and not required as part of the test, so that we can find and fix such tests in the future. 

Alternately, it is possible to fix the test to work with $tdir on some other MDT, but that is likely much more effort per test, and could be deferred so that we get this useful functionality landed sooner.

How about setting default layout on ROOT in system setup:
lfs setdirstripe -D -c1 -i0 <mount>
So directory creation is the same as before under ROOT, and we don't need to change test scripts?

Patch Set 9:

sanityn 40a failed because 'mkdir' and 'lfs mkdir' is different, the latter is an ioctl, and will stat parent directory first, which will cause conflict lock, It's better to test it in the old way.

--- Message (Andreas Dilger, 2021-05-13 08:03:43.000000000) ---
Patch Set 24: Code-Review+2

(1 comment)

--- Message (Hongchao Zhang, 2021-05-18 07:15:39.000000000) ---
Patch Set 24: Code-Review+1

--- Message (Andreas Dilger, 2021-06-21 09:41:52.000000000) ---
Patch Set 24:

Lai,
this patch sets the default layout on the root directory, but (AFAIK) it does not allow inheritance of this layout elsewhere in the filesystem.  We need a follow-on patch to inherit the root layout for subdirectories, without explicitly setting it on every subdirectory, so that manual MDT balancing is no longer needed.

--- Message (Lai Siyao, 2021-06-21 09:49:26.000000000) ---
Patch Set 24:

Do you mean it will act like the default LOV on ROOT, which acts as the system default LOV?

--- Message (Andreas Dilger, 2021-06-21 10:54:51.000000000) ---
Patch Set 24:

Yes, something like that.  I'm not 100% sure if we can just make it take over the whole filesystem directory layout, or if there needs to be an extra "really make this the filesystem-wide default layout" flag.  I guess that would be the same as "--max-inherit=-1" to inherit this layout (virtually) across all subdirectories in the filesystem (it would not make an actual copy of the default layout in this case).

The main question is whether users/admins would be upset if suddenly *all* of their subdirectories would become remote on existing filesystems where MDT0000 is much more full than other MDTs?  Some admins may want to set "-D -c 1 -i -1" on the root to create new top-level subdirectories on new MDTs, but not affect leaf directories...   I guess if "--max-inherit=N" then that would mean "don't implicitly inherit the root layout for leaf directories that don't have it set on their parent".

--- Message (Lai Siyao, 2021-07-02 14:50:14.000000000) ---
Uploaded patch set 25: Patch Set 24 was rebased.

2 style warning(s).
